hero.title: Fresh herbs,
hero.description: Welcome to my (sorry, Cooper's 😅), garden, where our herbs and fruit are grown with more enthusiasm than expertise and are then lovingly shared with our neighbours. No strings attached!

about.paragraph1: In 2024, life took a very unexpected turn. But what started out as a story of heartbreak turned into one of the most transformative years of my life. Not only did I learn what I'm truly capable of, but it brought me to Cooper; my adorable black Cocker Spaniel (you may have heard him 🙈), who ultimately led us here to Blackpaw Cottage - the first place in a very long time where I feel truly...home.
about.paragraph2:
I'm sure by now you can guess what (or rather, who) inspired the name Blackpaw Cottage - but what most don't know is that I'm the kind of dog mum who buys a house so her baby can have a big backyard to run around in. What I didn't account for, though, was that backyards don't magically look after themselves (shocking!) - especially not one lovingly created by someone who poured her heart and soul into it; from herbs and fruit trees to beautiful native flowers.

Over the past 12 months, I've been fumbling my way through figuring out how to keep it all alive - and through absolute luck (and a little naive optimism), my garden is not just surviving, it's somehow... thriving.
about.paragraph3:
I'm now growing a lot more than I can use and because my love language is acts of service, I shared a post in our Facebook group asking if anyone in our community would like some of my excess herbs. And to my delight - so many of you said yes!

I also love going the extra mile, especially if I think it'll make someone smile, so instead of just handing you a bunch of herbs, I had some cute gift tags designed so that I could leave little handwritten notes from Cooper and I.
about.paragraph4:
One of my closest friends has been watching all of this unfold and I mentioned recently how fun it would be to have a website my neighbours could "order from" instead of trying to keep track of all the comments and DMs each time I post.

He immediately took that idea and brought this amazing website to life for me - and so...here we are!

If you made it this far - wow! Thank you for taking the time to get to know me ♥️.

orchard.name: Lemon
orchard.description: Bright, juicy lemons from a mature tree. Great for cooking, drinks, and everything in between.
orchard.tip: Pick when fully yellow for maximum juice.

orchard.name: Lime
orchard.description: Small, fragrant limes perfect for Thai cooking, cocktails, or squeezing over just about anything.
orchard.tip: Pick when slightly soft to the touch for the best juice yield.

orchard.name: Orange
orchard.description: Yes, they're green — yes, they're ripe! In Queensland's warm climate, homegrown oranges often stay green even when they're perfectly sweet and ready to eat. Don't judge a citrus by its colour. 🍊

herbs.name: Basil
herbs.description: Large, fragrant leaves perfect for caprese, pesto, or tossing fresh into pasta. The Italian classic.
herbs.tip: Pick from the top to encourage bushy growth.

herbs.name: Rosemary
herbs.description: Woody stems full of piney aroma. Great for roasted meats, bread, or in a vase on the windowsill.
herbs.tip: Cut 4-6 inch sprigs, just above a leaf node.

herbs.name: Parsley
herbs.description: Bright, grassy flavor — far better fresh than dried. Goes with almost everything savory.
herbs.tip: Cut outer stems at the base to keep it producing.

herbs.name: Lemon Thyme
herbs.description: Tiny leaves with a citrusy twist on classic thyme. Wonderful with fish, poultry, or desserts.
herbs.tip: The flavor is most intense before it flowers.

herbs.name: Oregano
herbs.description: The backbone of Italian and Greek cooking. We harvested heavily this year — resting the plant.

herbs.name: Mint
herbs.description: Fresh, fragrant, and endlessly generous — mint is the herb that practically begs to be shared. Snip a sprig for your tea, your cooking, or a little bundle for a neighbour.
herbs.tip: A little goes a long way — use sparingly.
